hero.title: Instantly launch a watt-controlled session on your indoor home trainer
hero.description: BullWatt uses ERG mode, which automatically sets your resistance to a precise power target according to your cadence. This ensures a constant Watt effort and you are free to choose and change the Watt target.
features.title: Features
features.power.title: Precise Power Control
features.power.desc: Maintain exact wattage targets with our advanced ERG mode that automatically adjusts resistance based on your cadence.
features.fast.title: Lightning Fast
features.fast.desc: Get started instantly with no loading times. Our minimalistic design ensures you spend more time training and less time waiting.
features.sessions.title: Training sessions
features.sessions.desc: We propose a catalog of structurized sessions to improve your workouts.
training.title: Training Sessions
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: I was bored by long loading times in other apps (like Zwift, MyWhoosh, Rouvy...). My goal was to create a fast way to launch an activity and record it. For me, a good session means working on wattage zones, which is why wattage control is the only option.
faq.a2: Click on Start, then click on Connect. You can adjust your power with buttons or key arrows (up, down, left, right). That's it! Don't forget to click on Stop to store the activity.
faq.a3: Bluetooth FTMS (Fitness Machine Service) is a Bluetooth profile that enables standardized communication between fitness equipment and apps for data exchange and control.
faq.a4: Functional Threshold Power (FTP) is the highest average power a cyclist can sustain for approximately one hour, measured in watts. It's a key metric used to gauge a rider's fitness, track progress, and establish personalized training zones.
faq.q5: How to export the activity recorded?
faq.a5.html: You can find all your sessions stored in TCX format. The last sessions are registered in your browser; there is no server-side backup/save.
faq.a6: A computer with Google Chrome, a home trainer, or an exercise bike compatible with Bluetooth FTMS and with support for setting a target power (ERG mode = the resistance automatically changes based on your cadence to deliver constant wattage).
faq.q7: How to prevent my laptop/tablet from sleeping?
faq.a7: We use the same mechanism as YouTube to prevent sleeping. You need to keep the training tab visible. You can use the split-screen feature to watch YouTube or Netflix while training.
faq.q8: It doesn't detect my connected product!
faq.a8: Check the requirements. If your device meets the requirements and still doesn't work, please contact us for assistance.
faq.q9: Is there a free alternative to Zwift that doesn't require a subscription?
faq.a9: Yes! BullWatt is a 100% free indoor cycling app. Unlike Zwift or TrainerRoad, there are no monthly fees, no "free trial" limits, and no credit card required. It is designed for cyclists who want to train on their home trainer without the overhead of a expensive subscription.
faq.q10: How can I use my smart trainer in ERG mode without an app installation?
faq.a10: BullWatt runs directly in your Google Chrome browser using Web Bluetooth. This means you don't need to download or install any software. Simply open the website, connect your FTMS-compatible trainer, and start your ERG mode workout instantly on any PC, Mac, or Android device.
faq.q11: Can I use BullWatt on my phone or tablet?
faq.a11: Absolutely. As long as you use Google Chrome, BullWatt works on Android phones, tablets, and laptops. It leverages the Web Bluetooth API to talk directly to your bike, making it one of the most lightweight mobile training solutions available.
faq.q12: How do I connect my Domyos, Wahoo, or Tacx trainer to a browser?
faq.a12: BullWatt supports any smart trainer that uses the standard Bluetooth FTMS protocol. This includes popular models like the Wahoo KICKR, Tacx Neo/Flow, Elite Suito/Direto, and Decathlon Domyos bikes. If your trainer supports "Controllable" Bluetooth, it will work with BullWatt.
faq.a13: Yes. Just connect your Strava account from the homepage and each session will be automatically uploaded. Or after finishing your training session, you can export your data by TCX and upload it to Strava. This allows you to keep track of your fitness, mileage, and power data alongside your outdoor rides without needing a paid platform to manage the sync.
faq.q14: What is the best browser-based app for structured indoor cycling workouts?
faq.a14: BullWatt is specifically designed for structured training. It offers a catalog of pre-set sessions—from SST (Sweet Spot Training) to VO2 Max intervals—all controlled via ERG mode. It's the ideal tool for riders who want a "no-nonsense" workout player that loads in seconds.
faq.q15: Can I watch YouTube or Netflix while using BullWatt?
faq.a15: Yes! Because BullWatt is a web app, you can use split-screen mode or a second tab to watch your favorite shows or races. BullWatt includes a "Wake Lock" feature (similar to YouTube) to ensure your laptop doesn't go to sleep while you are in the middle of a hard interval.
faq.q16: Do I need an ANT+ dongle to use BullWatt?
faq.a16: No. BullWatt uses native Bluetooth through your browser. As long as your device (laptop/phone) has Bluetooth enabled and you are using Chrome, you can connect directly to your smart trainer without any extra dongles or hardware.
faq.q17: How do I set my FTP in BullWatt?
faq.a17: On the BullWatt home page, you can easily input your FTP (Functional Threshold Power) in the "Setup Your FTP" box. All the catalog workouts will then automatically scale the resistance to your specific fitness level, ensuring you are training in the correct power zones.
faq.q18: Why should I use BullWatt instead of MyWhoosh or other free apps?
faq.a18: While other apps require huge downloads (often 10GB+) and high-end graphics cards, BullWatt is instant. There is no registration, no loading screens, and no heavy 3D graphics. It is the fastest way to get on your bike and start a high-quality, power-regulated workout.

footer.tagline: Instant power control for indoor cycling enthusiasts.
article.production-information.p1: BullWatt is a web-based indoor cycling platform designed for athletes who prioritize minimalist, high-efficiency training without the burden of subscriptions or software downloads.
article.production-information.p2: By utilizing Google Chrome and Bluetooth FTMS, the tool enables users to connect directly to smart trainers to execute precise, power-controlled workouts via ERG mode.
article.production-information.p3: The service offers a diverse catalog of structured sessions, ranging from brief intervals to long-distance endurance rides, all scaled to the user's Functional Threshold Power (FTP). Because it runs in a browser, it eliminates loading times and allows cyclists to easily multi-task with entertainment while their performance data is recorded. Users can seamlessly export their activities or sync them with Strava to maintain a comprehensive history of their fitness progress.
article.production-information.p4: Ultimately, it serves as a free, lightweight alternative to traditional cycling apps for those seeking a straightforward and functional workout experience.
article.compatible-hardware.h1: A Comprehensive List of FTMS devices for Indoor Cycling
article.compatible-hardware.p1.html: BullWatt is compatible with any smart trainer or exercise bike that uses the standard Bluetooth FTMS protocol and supports "Controllable" Bluetooth for setting a target power (ERG mode).
article.compatible-hardware.p2: The app provides a specific list of known supported devices, which includes:
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.intro: BullWatt allows you to train indoors without the need for a subscription. By leveraging web technologies and Bluetooth FTMS, you can connect directly to your smart trainer and perform structured workouts without any recurring fees. This guide will walk you through the steps to get started and make the most of your indoor cycling sessions.
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.h1: What you don't have to worry about
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.list1.h:
• No Subscription Fees: BullWatt is completely free to use, allowing you to access all features without any recurring costs.
  • No Software Downloads: Since BullWatt runs in your web browser, there's no need to download or install any software on your device.
  • No Complex Setup: Connecting to your smart trainer is straightforward with Bluetooth FTMS, and the app guides you through the process.
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.p1.html: You can train indoors on the web without a subscription by using BullWatt, a 100% free indoor cycling app that requires no monthly fees, credit cards, or account registrations. Because it runs natively in your web browser, there is no need to download or install any heavy software.
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.p2: Here is exactly how you can set it up and start training:
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.step1.d: You will need a device (PC, Mac or tablet) running Google Chrome with Bluetooth enabled. You also need a smart trainer or exercise bike that supports the standard Bluetooth FTMS protocol and ERG mode, such as popular models from Wahoo, Tacx, Elite, or Zwift Hub. It connects directly via native Bluetooth, so you do not need an ANT+ dongle.
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.step2.d: Open the BullWatt homepage and enter your Functional Threshold Power (FTP) into the "Setup Your FTP" box. This step is crucial because it allows the app to automatically scale the resistance of the catalog workouts to your specific fitness level.
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.step3.d: Choose a structured workout from the catalog. The app offers a variety of sessions, ranging from quick 16-minute "Flash Burns" to VO2 Max intervals, Sweet Spot Training (SST), and longer endurance rides inspired by famous climbs like Alpe d'Huez and Mont Ventoux. Alternatively, you can select a "Free session" to manually manage your own power targets.
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.step4.d: Click Connect to pair your smart trainer using Web Bluetooth. Once connected, BullWatt uses ERG mode to automatically adjust your trainer's resistance based on your cadence, ensuring you hit the exact wattage targets for your workout. If needed, you can manually adjust the power using your device's arrow keys or on-screen buttons.
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.step5.d: When your workout is complete, be sure to click Stop to store the activity. BullWatt does not use server-side backups, but your recent sessions are saved locally in your browser. You can export your workout as a TCX file or set up an automatic sync to upload your ride directly to Strava.
article.bullwatt-no-subscription.bonus: As an added bonus, because BullWatt operates in a browser and features a "Wake Lock" to prevent your screen from sleeping, you can easily use split-screen mode to watch YouTube or Netflix while you train.
article.bullwatt-vs-zwift.h1: Some people pay to ride indoor
article.bullwatt-vs-zwift.p1: Many cyclists choose platforms like Zwift, which require a subscription fee, to access a wide range of virtual routes, structured workouts, and social features. These services offer a polished experience with extensive community engagement and gamification elements.
article.bullwatt-vs-zwift.p2: Others prefer to use open-source or free alternatives like BullWatt, which allow them to train without recurring costs. These platforms focus on core training functionalities, privacy, and community-driven development, providing a minimalist yet effective indoor cycling experience.
article.bullwatt-vs-zwift.h2: Some people wait to ride indoor
article.bullwatt-vs-zwift.p3: Have you ever faced the frustration of waiting for a software update or dealing with connectivity issues during your indoor training sessions? Open-source platforms like BullWatt eliminate these barriers, allowing you to focus on your workout without interruptions.
article.bullwatt-vs-zwift.p4.html: Choosing an open-source, web-based application like BullWatt over a heavy platform like Zwift comes down to speed, simplicity, and cost. While Zwift is known for its gamified virtual 3D worlds, BullWatt is specifically designed for cyclists who want a frictionless, "no-nonsense" structured workout.
article.bullwatt-vs-zwift.p5: Here are the primary advantages of choosing BullWatt:
article.bullwatt-vs-zwift.list.html:
• Completely Free: Unlike Zwift, which requires a paid subscription, BullWatt is a 100% free indoor cycling app. There are no monthly fees, no credit cards required, and no limits tied to free trials.
  • Zero Downloads or Installations: Apps like Zwift and MyWhoosh often require massive downloads (sometimes 10GB+) and high-end graphics cards. BullWatt bypasses this entirely by running natively in your Google Chrome browser using Web Bluetooth. You can start a session instantly on a PC, Mac, or Android device without downloading any software.
  • No Loading Screens or Registration: The creator built BullWatt directly to solve the frustration of long loading times on other apps. It features a minimalist design with no loading times, no ads, and no user registration required. It is the fastest way to get on your bike and start training.
  • Pure Focus on Power-Regulated Training: Instead of navigating a virtual world, BullWatt focuses strictly on precise power control using ERG mode. It automatically adjusts your smart trainer's resistance based on your cadence and your personal Functional Threshold Power (FTP) to keep you in the correct wattage zones. It also includes a robust catalog of pre-set sessions, from Sweet Spot Training (SST) to VO2 Max intervals.
  • Built for Multitasking: Because it is a lightweight web app, BullWatt is ideal for riders who prefer their own entertainment. You can easily use split-screen mode to watch Netflix or YouTube while you train. The app includes a "Wake Lock" feature to ensure your screen doesn't go to sleep in the middle of a hard interval.
  • Hardware Simplicity: You do not need an ANT+ dongle or extra hardware to use the app. As long as you have Bluetooth enabled on your device and use Chrome, it connects directly to standard FTMS-compatible smart trainers.
  • Data Privacy and Open-Source Transparency: BullWatt does not rely on server-side backups; your recent sessions are simply stored locally within your browser. You can easily export your workouts as TCX files or auto-sync them to Strava to track your fitness alongside outdoor rides. Furthermore, its codebase is hosted on a GitHub repository, reflecting its open-source and community-accessible nature.
article.bullwatt-vs-zwift.conclusion.htm: Ultimately, if you want to avoid expensive overhead and heavy 3D graphics in favor of instant, high-quality, power-regulated workouts, BullWatt provides a streamlined alternative to Zwift.
article.hardware-zwift-slow.intro: For many indoor cyclists, virtual training platforms have become a double-edged sword. While navigating digital landscapes can be entertaining, the technical requirements to run these applications smoothly have skyrocketed. If you do not have a top-tier gaming computer, trying to log a simple workout can quickly become a frustrating experience.
article.hardware-zwift-slow.h1: The Heavy Toll of 3D Cycling Worlds
article.hardware-zwift-slow.p1: Applications like Zwift and MyWhoosh rely on heavy 3D graphics to render their virtual environments. Because there is a massive amount of data to load, these applications require huge downloads, often exceeding 10GB, and demand high-end graphics cards to function properly.
article.hardware-zwift-slow.p2: These robust 3D worlds are incredibly resource-intensive. Instead of simply pedaling, users with older or standard PCs are forced to endure frustratingly long loading times just to get their software up and running. For cyclists who just want to train, this bloat is a major barrier to entry.
article.hardware-zwift-slow.h2: The BullWatt Solution: Fast, Simple, and Focused
article.hardware-zwift-slow.p3: For users who do not have a powerful PC configuration (or simply want to bypass the heavy loading screens), BullWatt offers a lightweight experience.
article.hardware-zwift-slow.p4: BullWatt completely eliminates the need for high-end hardware because it features no heavy 3D graphics and requires zero downloads.
article.hardware-zwift-slow.p5: Instead of acting as a standalone, resource-heavy application, BullWatt runs natively directly within your Google Chrome browser using Web Bluetooth.
article.hardware-zwift-slow.p6: By ditching the virtual world, BullWatt provides a "no-nonsense" approach that is dedicated entirely to the practice of cycling. This design philosophy results in several distinct advantages:
article.hardware-zwift-slow.list.html:
• Zero Loading Times: Beyond the initial fraction of a second it takes to load the webpage, there is no waiting around. You can instantly connect your smart trainer and launch into your session.
  • Minimalist Efficiency: By design, BullWatt is simple and requires no registration. It is built specifically to be a lightning-fast way to launch an activity and record it.
  • Hardware Friendly: Because it is incredibly lightweight, it works flawlessly on almost any device capable of running Chrome, including older laptops, Android phones, and tablets.
article.hardware-zwift-slow.conclusion: You do not need an expensive, high-powered gaming rig to get a high-quality, structured indoor cycling workout. By focusing purely on precise power control and ERG mode rather than resource-heavy 3D rendering, BullWatt provides an instant, frictionless training experience that lets you spend more time sweating on the bike and less time waiting for your PC to load.
